findings.summary: Removing recover_missing_final from the events endpoint leaves final-recovery retries without a normal caller.
Dropping provider finals when turn_id is missing can lose completions after restart because active_turn_id is in-memory.

expected.notes: Human-accepted useful findings from the prior review bot for the first PR commit.

input.body:
## Summary
- stop event polling from running narrator final recovery
- remove transcript and legacy final recovery paths that guessed missing finals
- add a turn lifecycle service that owns durable turn start and provider-final recording
- make provider final handling idempotent per turn and recover missing in-memory active turn state from the latest active stored turn
- keep recovery explicit and turn-bound via POST /api/narrator/sessions/{session_id}/recover-final

## Root cause
The runaway narrator loop came from recovery code that could mutate state during event reads and emit recovered finals without a turn id. Those unkeyed finals bypassed the one-final-per-turn guard and could be recreated on repeated client polling.

## Design direction
This PR moves final-turn ownership out of ad hoc provider event callbacks and into a small application-layer lifecycle service. Provider finals now have to resolve to an active stored turn before narrator final generation runs. Duplicate provider finals for the same turn reuse the already-recorded provider final instead of creating new final state.

## Validation
- uv run ruff check server friday agent
- uv run mypy
- git diff --check
input.diff:
diff --git a/friday/application/narrator_recovery.py b/friday/application/narrator_recovery.py
index 04297a7..6c99bf5 100644
--- a/friday/application/narrator_recovery.py
+++ b/friday/application/narrator_recovery.py
@@ -2,15 +2,10 @@
 
 from __future__ import annotations
 
-import logging
 from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable
-from datetime import UTC, datetime
 
-from friday.domain.provider import Provider
 from friday.domain.repositories import NarratorRepository, StoredNarratorEvent, StoredSession
 
-logger = logging.getLogger("friday.narrator")
-
 _FINAL_RECOVERY_MIN_AGE_SECS = 15.0
 
 
@@ -19,15 +14,12 @@ class NarratorRecoveryService:
         self,
         *,
         store: NarratorRepository,
-        require_provider: Callable[[str], Provider],
         emit_final_for_text: Callable[..., Awaitable[StoredNarratorEvent | None]],
     ) -> None:
         self._store = store
-        self._require_provider = require_provider
         self._emit_final_for_text = emit_final_for_text
 
     async def recover_missing_final(self, stored: StoredSession) -> None:
-        await self._recover_provider_finals_from_transcript(stored)
         for turn in self._store.recoverable_turns(
             session_id=stored.id,
             min_provider_final_age_seconds=_FINAL_RECOVERY_MIN_AGE_SECS,
@@ -45,97 +37,5 @@ class NarratorRecoveryService:
                 },
             )
 
-        latest_provider_final = next(
-            (
-                event
-                for event in reversed(
-                    self._store.list_provider_events(session_id=stored.id, limit=50)
-                )
-                if event.type == "final" and event.summary and event.summary.strip()
-            ),
-            None,
-        )
-        if latest_provider_final is None:
-            return
-        if not latest_provider_final.summary:
-            return
-        final_age_secs = (datetime.now(tz=UTC) - latest_provider_final.created_at).total_seconds()
-        if final_age_secs < _FINAL_RECOVERY_MIN_AGE_SECS:
-            return
-        has_narrator_final = any(
-            event.type == "final" and event.created_at >= latest_provider_final.created_at
-            for event in self._store.list_narrator_events(
-                session_id=stored.id,
-                after_id=0,
-                limit=200,
-            )
-        )
-        if has_narrator_final:
-            return
-        logger.warning(
-            "recovering legacy missing narrator final | session=%s provider_event=%s",
-            stored.id,
-            latest_provider_final.id,
-        )
-        await self._emit_final_for_text(
-            stored,
-            final_text=latest_provider_final.summary,
-            turn_id=None,
-            source="narrator_final_recovery",
-            extra_payload={
-                "provider_session_id": stored.provider_session_id,
-                "recovered_provider_event_id": latest_provider_final.id,
-            },
-        )
-
-    async def _recover_provider_finals_from_transcript(self, stored: StoredSession) -> None:
-        missing_turns = self._store.turns_missing_provider_final(
-            session_id=stored.id,
-            min_age_seconds=_FINAL_RECOVERY_MIN_AGE_SECS,
-        )
-        if not missing_turns:
-            return
-        provider = self._require_provider(stored.harness)
-        try:
-            transcript = await provider.get_transcript(stored.provider_session_id)
-        except Exception as err:
-            logger.warning(
-                "failed to recover provider transcript | session=%s err=%s",
-                stored.id,
-                err,
-            )
-            return
-        assistant_messages = [
-            message
-            for message in transcript
-            if message.role == "assistant"
-            and message.text.strip()
-            and message.completed_at is not None
-        ]
-        for turn in missing_turns:
-            message = next(
-                (
-                    candidate
-                    for candidate in assistant_messages
-                    if candidate.completed_at is not None
-                    and candidate.completed_at >= turn.created_at
-                ),
-                None,
-            )
-            if message is None:
-                continue
-            provider_event = self._store.append_provider_event(
-                session_id=stored.id,
-                provider_session_id=stored.provider_session_id,
-                event_type="final",
-                summary=message.text,
-                payload={"turn_id": turn.id, "source": "provider_transcript_recovery"},
-            )
-            self._store.mark_turn_provider_final(
-                turn_id=turn.id,
-                provider_final_text=message.text,
-                provider_final_event_id=provider_event.id,
-            )
-
 
 __all__ = ["NarratorRecoveryService"]
diff --git a/server/app/main.py b/server/app/main.py
index 925cf30..9053fa9 100644
--- a/server/app/main.py
+++ b/server/app/main.py
@@ -436,7 +436,6 @@ def create_app() -> FastAPI:
     ) -> NarratorEventsResponse:
         manager = get_narrator_manager(fastapi_request)
         try:
-            await manager.recover_missing_final(session_id)
             events = manager.list_events(
                 session_id=session_id,
                 after_id=max(after_id, 0),
diff --git a/server/app/narrator.py b/server/app/narrator.py
index 2c6020c..a2b4b08 100644
--- a/server/app/narrator.py
+++ b/server/app/narrator.py
@@ -77,7 +77,6 @@ class NarratorManager:
         )
         self._recovery = NarratorRecoveryService(
             store=store,
-            require_provider=self._require_provider,
             emit_final_for_text=self._emit_final_for_text,
         )
 
@@ -333,6 +332,13 @@ class NarratorManager:
         source: str,
         extra_payload: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
     ) -> StoredNarratorEvent | None:
+        if turn_id is None:
+            logger.warning(
+                "dropping narrator final without turn id | session=%s source=%s",
+                stored.id,
+                source,
+            )
+            return None
         snapshot = self._snapshot_builder.build(
             stored,
             decision_type="final_response",

## Summary
- stop event polling from running narrator final recovery
- remove transcript and legacy final recovery paths that guessed missing finals
- add a turn lifecycle service that owns durable turn start and provider-final recording
- make provider final handling idempotent per turn and recover missing in-memory active turn state from the latest active stored turn

## Root cause
The runaway narrator loop came from recovery code that could mutate state during event reads and emit recovered finals without a turn id. Those unkeyed finals bypassed the one-final-per-turn guard and could be recreated on repeated client polling.

## Design direction

Provider finals now have to resolve to an active stored turn before narrator final generation runs. Duplicate provider finals for the same turn reuse the already-recorded provider final instead of creating new final state.

## Validation
- uv run ruff check server friday agent
- uv run mypy
- git diff --check
